steps.title: Divide both sides by 15 to simplify
steps.content:
The equation $15x^2 = 15(9)$ has a common factor of 15 on both sides. Divide both sides by 15:
$$x^2 = 9$$

steps.title: Take the square root to find both solutions
steps.content:
Taking the square root of both sides:
$$x = \pm\sqrt{9} = \pm 3$$
The two solutions are $x = 3$ and $x = -3$. Since the problem asks for the negative solution, the answer is $-3$.
